subsections.text: Except as otherwise provided in subsection 2 and NRS 239.0115 , information which is maintained in the system created pursuant to NRS 485.313 is confidential.
The Department may only disclose information which is maintained in the system to:

subsections.label: a
subsections.text: A state or local governmental agency for the purpose of enforcing NRS 485.185 , including investigating or litigating a violation or alleged violation;
With whom the Department has contracted to provide services relating to the system created pursuant to NRS 485.313 ; and
To whom the information is disclosed only pursuant to a nondisclosure or confidentiality agreement which relates to the information;

subsections.label: d
subsections.text: A person who requests information regarding his or her own status;

subsections.label: e
subsections.text: The parent or legal guardian of the person about whom the information is requested if the person is an unemancipated minor or legally incapacitated;

subsections.label: f
subsections.text: A person who has a power of attorney from the person about whom the information is requested;

subsections.label: g
subsections.text: A person who submits a notarized release from the person about whom the information is requested which is dated no more than 90 days before the date of the request; or

subsections.label: h
subsections.text: A person who has suffered a loss or injury in a crash involving a motor vehicle, or the person’s authorized insurer or a representative of the authorized insurer, who requests:
Information for use in the crash report; and
For each motor vehicle involved in the crash:
(I) The name and address of each registered owner;
(II) The name of the insurer; and
(III) The number of the policy of liability insurance.
A person who knowingly violates the provisions of this section is guilty of a category D felony and shall be punished as provided in NRS 193.130 .
As used in this section, “authorized insurer” has the meaning ascribed to it in NRS 679A.030 .
